text:
Admission Date:  1975-12-17              Discharge Date:   2005-08-15

Date of Birth:  2007-02-16             Sex:   M

Service: ORTHOPAEDICS

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:William
Chief Complaint:
Crush injury to pelvis

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
ORIF pelvic ring fracture


History of Present Illness:
51 year old male had 200lbs conveyor belt 19710 feet onto
patients pelvis area pinning him to the ground.  Took six
workers to free him.  Patient seen at OSH and was alert and
oriented X 3 at time of presentation.  Subsequently dropped
systolic pressures to the 90's and tachy in the 100's. at which
time he received 2 L of NS for resusitation.  Pt transferred to
St. Joseph Regional Health Center and found to be lethargic in ED.  Minimally following
commands and intubated for airway protection.  Patient
transfused 1 unit PRBC in ED for Hct of 32.1 on suspicion of
bleeding into pelvis.

Past Medical History:
none

Family History:
N/C

Physical Exam:
97.1 96/53 72 16 700%
intubated, sedated
PERRLA
RRR

Bilateral upper extremity no gross deformities, skin intact,
good pulses distally.
Bilateral lower extremtiy no gross deformities, skin intact, leg
lengths equal, 2+ DP bilaterally

1977-06-02 03:53AM BLOOD Calcium-7.5* Phos-2.4* Mg-1.8

Brief Hospital Course:
Mr. Brown was taken to the St. Joseph Regional Health Center ED from an outside hospital
and admitted to the Trauma service.  In the ED, patient was
given a unit of blood and intubated due to lethargy and the need
for airway protection.  He had a CT of the
head/chest/abd/pelvis.  CXR was negative for pneumothorax or rib
fracture.  The CT of the abdomen and pelvis showed a right
sacral fracture with superior displcement of right hemipelvis.
Left pubic bone crush fracture, a right sided hematoma and a
right L5 and left L2 transverse process fracture.  CT of the
head was negative.  C-collar was maintained and he was admitted
to the Truama/SICU for further management.
In the SICU he was hemodynamically stable and an A-line was
placed.  He was able to follow simple commands but remaind
intubated.  He was made NPO and taken to the OR later that day
for ORIF of his pelvic ring fracture.
There were no perioperative complications and he was
hemodynamically stable at all times.  He was transferred to the
PACU for recovery and had an appropriate post-operative check.
He was transferred to the floor for further management.
He was seen by spine and vascular who paid particular attention
to his neuro exam and pulses, respecitvely.  There was a brief
decrease in pulses on the right lower extremity over the left
lower extremity the evening of HD2 but resolved without
intervention.  Spine was able to clear his spine HD 2 and
recommended conservative management for spinal issues.  No brace
was warranted at that time.
Lovenox was started HD2 after his trauma issue were cleared and
it will continue for 6 weeks at 40mg per day.
HD 3 repeat pelvic CT was obtained which showed proper screw
placement and closure of the sacral fractures.  However, on HD 4
we ordered an AP pelvis and identified an area of the ishial
ring that was displaced posteriorly.  He was informed and
consented in the usual manner and was taken back to the OR for
ORIF of this area. He had no perioperative complications and was
transferred back to the floor for further management.
He was seen by PT each day and made improvement on range of
motion and strength.  He is to be non-weight bearing to the
right lower extremity but may sit up in bed.  At the time of
discharge he was allowed to pivot on his left foot for
transfers.  He is to follow with Dr. Curry in his clinic.
Lovenox is to be continued for 6 weeks.  He was discharged in
stable condition.

Medications on Admission:
none

1. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: 1-2 Tablets PO Q4-6H (every
4 to 6 hours) as needed.

8. Lovenox 30 mg/0.3 mL Syringe Sig: One (1) syringe
Subcutaneous twice a day for 6 weeks.

Discharge Instructions:
Lumbar corset for comfort.  Please remain non-weight bearing on
the right lower extremtiy.  You may use crutches for ambulation.
 Continue the Lovenox injections for 6 weeks.  Pain medication
as needeed.

Followup Instructions:
Please follow up with Dr. Curry in two weeks.  Call 454-713-5842.
